# growthbook_segment (Resource)

A GrowthBook segment. Segments define a subset of users to filter experiment and metric analyses, either via a SQL query (`SQL`) or a fact table with filters (`FACT`).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "growthbook_segment" "active_paid" {
  name            = "Active paid users"
  type            = "SQL"
  datasource_id   = "ds_warehouse"
  identifier_type = "user_id"
  sql             = "SELECT user_id, date FROM subscriptions WHERE status = 'active'"
  owner           = "data-team"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `datasource_id` (String) ID of the data source this segment belongs to.
- `identifier_type` (String) Identifier type the segment applies to (e.g. `user`, `anonymous`).
- `name` (String) Human-readable segment name.
- `type` (String) Segment type: `SQL` (query-defined) or `FACT` (fact table + filters).

### Optional

- `description` (String) Optional description of the segment.
- `fact_table_id` (String) ID of the fact table backing the segment. Required for `FACT` segments.
- `filters` (List of String) Fact table filter IDs that further constrain a `FACT` segment.
- `managed_by` (String) Where the segment is managed from: empty (anywhere) or `api`.
- `owner` (String) User ID or email of the segment owner.
- `projects` (List of String) Project IDs that can access this segment.
- `query` (String) SQL query that defines the segment. Required for `SQL` segments.

### Read-Only

- `date_created` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `date_updated` (String) Last update timestamp.
- `id` (String) Unique segment identifier (e.g. `seg_...`).
